codeReview.summary: The feature mostly swaps admin pages from generic Tailwind colors to shared tokens, but it does not fully align the admin surface to the shared design system. Several admin surfaces still use legacy hard-coded utility styling for the login gradient/background and multiple controls/cards continue using non-token radius and shadow utilities, so the implementation falls short of the stated expected behavior.

issues.description: The admin login screen still uses hard-coded color utilities (`bg-gradient-to-br from-blue-50 to-cyan-50`) instead of shared semantic tokens, so the admin pages do not consistently use the same design token system as required.

The login card keeps `shadow-xl` rather than a shared design-system shadow token (`shadow-card`/`shadow-elevated`), so buttons, inputs, and cards are not fully aligned to the shared admin/user design system.

Sidebar navigation items still use `rounded-lg` instead of the shared radius token (`rounded-card` or another documented token), leaving admin controls only partially standardized on the shared border-radius system.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `ui-worker` skill requires browser-based visual verification for every feature, but related worker transcripts in this milestone show browser verification can be blocked by auth flows and workers may fall back to code inspection while still reporting verification success. The skill should clarify what evidence is acceptable when authenticated routes block direct visual verification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/wendy/wcstubhub-clone/.factory/skills/ui-worker/SKILL.md` Step 6 says every feature must be visually verified with agent-browser. In `/Users/wendy/.factory/missions/bc99579a-c2db-4a67-aad4-2d259a3c559d/worker-transcripts.jsonl` around the `product-event-detail-polish` session, the worker reports browser verification but ultimately falls back to code review because authenticated pages redirected to login.

summary: Review failed: the commit updates many admin colors to semantic tokens, but it does not complete the token refresh promised by the feature. The admin login page still uses non-token gradient colors and non-token shadow styling, and some shared admin controls still use legacy radius utilities, so the admin panel is only partially aligned to the shared design system.
